mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing borrowing costs and boosting equity valuations, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets, increasing corporate earnings potential and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low UNRATE regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which correlates with stronger corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO upturns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high CPI regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and housing starts signals robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-expansion regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.15 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates combined with rising inflation signal monetary tightening, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings pressure.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in tightening regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.20 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k signals labor market strength, increasing consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high PAYEMS regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential mortgage credit availability (RSAFS) indicates improved housing market liquidity, boosting consumer wealth and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in RSAFS upturns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year windows.
